title: sphaerostephanos invisus
intro: Sphaerostephanos invisus is a species of fern in the family Drynariaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, where it grows as an epiphyte on tree trunks. Traditionally, Sphaerostephanos invisus has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that can be found in herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions.
